WE: H. Meurig Evans and W.O. Thomas: Welsh-English, English-Welsh Dictionary (1969)
EIE: J.P. Mallory and D.Q. Adams, eds. Encyclopedia of Indo-European Culture (1997)
DEO: Hermann Vinterberg and C.A. Bodelsen: Dansk-Engelsk Ordbog (1966)
LD: Bronius Piesarskas and Bronius Svecevicius: Lithuanian Dictionary (1994)
ELD: Charlton T. Lewis: An Elementary Latin Dictionary (1999)
LS: Liddell and Scott: Greek-English Lexicon, 7th-9th ed's (1882-1940), rev.
IOI: Ruth P.M. and Winfred P. Lehmann: An Introduction to Old Irish (1975)
GED: Winfred P. Lehmann: A Gothic Etymological Dictionary (1986)
ODE: C.T. Onions: The Oxford Dictionary of English Etymology (1966)
TPE: Lee M. Hollander: The Poetic Edda (1962)
R1: Josette Rey-Debove and Alain Rey, eds. Le Nouveau Petit Robert (1993)
ASD: Joseph Bosworth and T. Northcote Toller: An Anglo-Saxon Dictionary (1898)
ICE: Richard Cleasby and Gudbrand Vigfusson: An Icelandic-English Dictionary (1874)
HGE: Vladimir E. Orel: A Handbook of Germanic Etymology (2003)
IED: Patrick S. Dinneen: An Irish-English Dictionary (1927)
TLL: Frederick Bodmer: The Loom of Language (1944)
MED: Robert E. Lewis et al., eds. Middle English Dictionary (1954-1999, 2001)
LRC: Linguistics Research Center, University of Texas, Austin
AHD: Calvert Watkins: The American Heritage Dictionary of Indo-European Roots, 2nd ed. (2000)
CDC: W.D. Whitney and B.E. Smith: The Century Dictionary and Cyclopedia (1889-1911)
Sal: Diccionario Salamanca de la Lengua Española (1996)
W2I: Webster's New International Dictionary of the English Language, 2nd ed. (1959)
MEV: J.R.R. Tolkien: A Middle English Vocabulary (1922)
OED: James A.H. Murray et al: The Oxford English Dictionary (1933)
